output:
1	---
     2	name: cron-scheduling
     3	description: Schedule and manage recurring tasks with cron and systemd timers. Use when setting up cron jobs, writing systemd timer units, handling timezone-aware scheduling, monitoring failed jobs, implementing retry patterns, or debugging why a scheduled task didn't run.
     4	metadata: {"clawdbot":{"emoji":"⏰","requires":{"anyBins":["crontab","systemctl","at"]},"os":["linux","darwin"]}}
     5	---
     6	
     7	## Deepx (this repo)
     8	
     9	When saving artifacts for the user, prefer **`/_outputs/...`**. File tools use paths under **`/`** (repository root). This demo orchestrator can follow this skill directly for scheduling guidance without delegating to a subagent.
    10	
    11	# Cron & Scheduling
    12	
    13	Schedule and manage recurring tasks. Covers cron syntax, crontab management, systemd timers, one-off scheduling, timezone handling, monitoring, and common failure patterns.
    14	
    15	## When to Use
    16	
    17	- Running scripts on a schedule (backups, reports, cleanup)
    18	- Setting up systemd timers (modern cron alternative)
    19	- Debugging why a scheduled job didn't run
    20	- Handling timezones in scheduled tasks
    21	- Monitoring and alerting on job failures
    22	- Running one-off delayed commands
    23	
    24	## Cron Syntax
    25	
    26	### The five fields
    27	
    28	```
    29	┌───────── minute (0-59)
    30	│ ┌─────── hour (0-23)
    31	│ │ ┌───── day of month (1-31)
    32	│ │ │ ┌─── month (1-12 or JAN-DEC)
    33	│ │ │ │ ┌─ day of week (0-7, 0 and 7 = Sunday, or SUN-SAT)
    34	│ │ │ │ │
    35	* * * * * command
    36	```
    37	
    38	### Common schedules
    39	
    40	```bash
    41	# Every minute
    42	* * * * * /path/to/script.sh
    43	
    44	# Every 5 minutes
    45	*/5 * * * * /path/to/script.sh
    46	
    47	# Every hour at :00
    48	0 * * * * /path/to/script.sh
    49	
    50	# Every day at 2:30 AM
    51	30 2 * * * /path/to/script.sh
    52	
    53	# Every Monday at 9:00 AM
    54	0 9 * * 1 /path/to/script.sh
    55	
    56	# Every weekday at 8:00 AM
    57	0 8 * * 1-5 /path/to/script.sh
    58	
    59	# First day of every month at midnight
    60	0 0 1 * * /path/to/script.sh
    61	
    62	# Every 15 minutes during business hours (Mon-Fri 9-17)
    63	*/15 9-17 * * 1-5 /path/to/script.sh
    64	
    65	# Twice a day (9 AM and 5 PM)
    66	0 9,17 * * * /path/to/script.sh
    67	
    68	# Every quarter (Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct) on the 1st at midnight
    69	0 0 1 1,4,7,10 * /path/to/script.sh
    70	
    71	# Every Sunday at 3 AM
    72	0 3 * * 0 /path/to/script.sh
    73	```
    74	
    75	### Special strings (shorthand)
    76	
    77	```bash
    78	@reboot    /path/to/script.sh   # Run once at startup
    79	@yearly    /path/to/script.sh   # 0 0 1 1 *
    80	@monthly   /path/to/script.sh   # 0 0 1 * *
    81	@weekly    /path/to/script.sh   # 0 0 * * 0
    82	@daily     /path/to/script.sh   # 0 0 * * *
    83	@hourly    /path/to/script.sh   # 0 * * * *
    84	```
    85	
    86	## Crontab Management
    87	
    88	```bash
    89	# Edit current user's crontab
    90	crontab -e
    91	
    92	# List current crontab
    93	crontab -l
    94	
    95	# Edit another user's crontab (root)
    96	sudo crontab -u www-data -e
    97	
    98	# Remove all cron jobs (be careful!)
    99	crontab -r
   100	
   101	# Install crontab from file
   102	crontab mycrontab.txt
   103	
   104	# Backup crontab
   105	crontab -l > crontab-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d).txt
   106	```
   107	
   108	### Crontab best practices
   109	
   110	```bash
   111	# Set PATH explicitly (cron has minimal PATH)
   112	PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin
   113	
   114	# Set MAILTO for error notifications
   115	MAILTO=admin@example.com
   116	
   117	# Set shell explicitly
   118	SHELL=/bin/bash
   119	
   120	# Full crontab example
   121	PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin
   122	MAILTO=admin@example.com
   123	SHELL=/bin/bash
   124	
   125	# Backups
   126	0 2 * * * /opt/scripts/backup.sh >> /var/log/backup.log 2>&1
   127	
   128	# Cleanup old logs
   129	0 3 * * 0 find /var/log/myapp -name "*.log" -mtime +30 -delete
   130	
   131	# Health check
   132	*/5 * * * * /opt/scripts/healthcheck.sh || /opt/scripts/alert.sh "Health check failed"
   133	```
   134	
   135	## Systemd Timers
   136	
   137	### Create a timer (modern cron replacement)
   138	
   139	```ini
   140	# /etc/systemd/system/backup.service
   141	[Unit]
   142	Description=Daily backup
   143	
   144	[Service]
   145	Type=oneshot
   146	ExecStart=/opt/scripts/backup.sh
   147	User=backup
   148	StandardOutput=journal
   149	StandardError=journal
   150	```
   151	
   152	```ini
   153	# /etc/systemd/system/backup.timer
   154	[Unit]
   155	Description=Run backup daily at 2 AM
   156	
   157	[Timer]
   158	OnCalendar=*-*-* 02:00:00
   159	Persistent=true
   160	RandomizedDelaySec=300
   161	
   162	[Install]
   163	WantedBy=timers.target
   164	```
   165	
   166	```bash
   167	# Enable and start the timer
   168	sudo systemctl daemon-reload
   169	sudo systemctl enable --now backup.timer
   170	
   171	# Check timer status
   172	systemctl list-timers
   173	systemctl list-timers --all
   174	
   175	# Check last run
   176	systemctl status backup.service
   177	journalctl -u backup.service --since today
   178	
   179	# Run manually (for testing)
   180	sudo systemctl start backup.service
   181	
   182	# Disable timer
   183	sudo systemctl disable --now backup.timer
   184	```
   185	
   186	### OnCalendar syntax
   187	
   188	```ini
   189	# Systemd calendar expressions
   190	
   191	# Daily at midnight
   192	OnCalendar=daily
   193	# or: OnCalendar=*-*-* 00:00:00
   194	
   195	# Every Monday at 9 AM
   196	OnCalendar=Mon *-*-* 09:00:00
   197	
   198	# Every 15 minutes
   199	OnCalendar=*:0/15

[227 more lines — use offset=199 to continue]
